chemistry.reaction_name: Visible-light induced photocatalytic degradation of fluorescein
chemistry.mechanism_type: Photocatalytic degradation via reactive oxygen species (ROS)

photocatalyst.name: ZnO-APTMS-Au
photocatalyst.role: Photocatalyst for generating reactive oxygen species

mechanistic_details.key_intermediate: Orange intermediate degradation products (mentioned but not identified)

chemistry.scope_summary: Aqueous photocatalytic degradation of fluorescein using ZnO-APTMS-Au nanoparticles under visible light (400-700 nm) irradiation in a coiled-flow inverter reactor. The study achieved 40% degradation after 20 minutes and complete degradation within 4-6 hours at pH 6.5-7.0.

figures.key_information: Four different photoreactor configurations (A-D) shown with light bulb for scale reference. A) cylindrical reactor with rotating mechanism and external LEDs, B) spiral coil reactor with CFL illumination, C) traditional light bulb with heating element reactor, D) microreactor chip with central light source
figures.caption: Figure 1.  Lighting configurations for photoreactors. (A) Batch photoreactor, (B) continuous-flow photoreactor, (C) microphotoreactor, and( D) coiled-flow inverter presented herein. | Figure 2.  Modular platform for the study of coiled-flow inverters (CFI's) as photoreactors, continuous-flow setup, platform dimensions, and model CFI specifications.

figures.flow_path: inlet through reaction chamber to monitoring chamber to expansion chamber to outlet
figures.reactor_geometry: rectangular chamber with cylindrical CFI arm
figures.key_information: photochemical reactor with coiled flow inverter (CFI) for inline monitoring and analysis
figures.caption: Figure 1.  Lighting configurations for photoreactors. (A) Batch photoreactor, (B) continuous-flow photoreactor, (C) microphotoreactor, and( D) coiled-flow inverter presented herein. | Figure 2.  Modular platform for the study of coiled-flow inverters (CFI's) as photoreactors, continuous-flow setup, platform dimensions, and model CFI specifications.
Figure 3.  Fluorescence imaging for the visualization of spatial gradients in irradiance on the outer surface of the photo-CFI. Image analysis shown in red scale for each photograph.
Figure 4.  (A) Visible-light LED source with wide-angle chips; (B) measurements of light intensity at different locations inside the reaction chamber for uniaxial top-down irradiation.

Figure 5.  Degradation of fluorescein induced by visible light in the presence of ZnO-APTMS-Au. (A) Decay of fluorescence emission as a function of time. (B) Normalized fluorescein concentration as a function of time. (C) Proposed mechanism for electronic band catalysis with ZnO-APTMS-Au nanoparticles as photocatalyst.

figure_descriptions: Four different photoreactor configurations (A-D) shown with light bulb for scale reference
Modular platform for coiled-flow inverters as photoreactors with detailed dimensions and specifications

notes: This is a photocatalytic degradation study focused on flow reactor development rather than synthetic chemistry. No batch baseline comparison is provided. The paper emphasizes reactor engineering and photon efficiency rather than chemical yield optimization. Residence time not explicitly stated but can be calculated from reactor volume and flow rate.
